title: jasione crispa subsp. tristis
intro: Jasione crispa subsp. tristis, also known as the small blue thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Jasione crispa subsp. tristis include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a treatment for digestive problems. The herbal preparation of this plant typically involves drying the flowers and leaves, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
